sections.label: Section 226
sections.original: ἔπειτʼ ἀπὸ ταρροῦ τοὺς θεοὺς ὑπερφρονεῖς ,
sections.translation: And so you look down upon the gods from your basket , and not from the earth ?

sections.label: Section 227b
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ ἄν ποτε
sections.translation: For I should not have rightly discovered things celestial if I had not suspended the intellect , and

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: λεπτὴν καταμείξας ἐς τὸν ὅμοιον ἀέρα .
sections.translation: mixed the thought in a subtle form with its kindred air . But if , being on the ground , I speculated from below on things above , I should never have discovered them . For the earth forcibly attracts to itself the meditative moisture . Water-cresses also suffer the very same thing .

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: τί φῄς ;
sections.translation: What do you say ? Does meditation attract the moisture to the water-cresses ? Come then , my little Socrates , descend to me , that you may teach me those things , for the sake of which I have come .

sections.label: Section 239
sections.original: ἦλθες δὲ κατὰ τί ;
sections.translation: And for what did you come ?

sections.label: Section 239b
sections.original: βουλόμενος μαθεῖν λέγειν .
sections.translation: Wishing to learn to speak ; for by reason of usury , and most ill-natured creditors , I am pillaged and plundered , and have my goods seized for debt .

sections.label: Section 242
sections.original: πόθεν δʼ ὑπόχρεως σαυτὸν ἔλαθες γενόμενος ;
sections.translation: How did you get in debt without observing it ?

sections.label: Section 243
sections.original: νόσος μʼ ἐπέτριψεν ἱππικὴ δεινὴ φαγεῖν .
sections.translation: A horse-disease consumed me--terrible at eating . But teach me the other one of your two causes , that which pays nothing ; and I will swear by the gods , I will pay down to you whatever reward you exact of me .

sections.label: Section 247
sections.original: ποίους θεοὺς ὀμεῖ σύ ; πρῶτον γὰρ θεοὶ
sections.translation: By what gods will you swear ? For , in the first place , gods are not a current coin with us .

sections.label: Section 248b
sections.original: τῷ γὰρ ὄμνυτʼ ; ἢ
sections.translation: By what do you swear ? By iron money , as in Byzantium ?

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: βούλει τὰ θεῖα πράγματʼ εἰδέναι σαφῶς
sections.translation: Do you wish to know clearly celestial matters , what they rightly are ?

sections.label: Section 251b
sections.original: νὴ Δίʼ εἴπερ ἔστι γε .
sections.translation: Yes , by Jupiter , if it be possible !

sections.label: Section 252
sections.original: καὶ ξυγγενέσθαι ταῖς Νεφέλαισιν ἐς λόγους ,
sections.translation: And to hold converse with the Clouds , our divinities ?

sections.label: Section 253b
sections.original: μάλιστά γε .
sections.translation: By all means .

sections.label: Section 254
sections.original: κάθιζε τοίνυν ἐπὶ τὸν ἱερὸν σκίμποδα .
sections.translation: Seat yourself , then , upon the sacred couch .

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: ἰδοὺ κάθημαι .
sections.translation: Well , I am seated !

sections.label: Section 255b
sections.original: τουτονὶ τοίνυν λαβὲ
sections.translation: Take , then , this chaplet .

sections.label: Section 256b
sections.original: ἐπὶ τί στέφανον ; οἴμοι Σώκρατες
sections.translation: For what purpose a chaplet ? Ah me ! Socrates , see that you do not sacrifice me like Athamas !

sections.label: Section 258
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ ταῦτα πάντα τοὺς τελουμένους
sections.translation: No ; we do all these to those who get initiated .

sections.label: Section 259b
sections.original: εἶτα δὴ τί κερδανῶ ;
sections.translation: Then what shall I gain , pray ?

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: λέγειν γενήσει τρῖμμα κρόταλον παιπάλη .
sections.translation: You shall become in oratory a tricky knave , a thorough rattle , a subtle speaker . But keep quiet .

sections.label: Section 261b
sections.original: μὰ τὸν Δίʼ οὐ ψεύσει γέ με ·
sections.translation: By Jupiter ! You will not deceive me ; for if I am besprinkled , I shall become fine flour .

sections.label: Section 263
sections.original: εὐφημεῖν χρὴ τὸν πρεσβύτην καὶ τῆς εὐχῆς ἐπακούειν .
sections.translation: It becomes the old man to speak words of good omen , and to hearken to my prayer . O sovereign King , immeasurable Air , who keepest the earth suspended , and through bright Aether , and ye august goddesses , the Clouds , sending thunder and lightning , arise , appear in the air , O mistresses , to your deep thinker !

sections.label: Section 267
sections.original: μήπω μήπω γε πρὶν ἂν τουτὶ πτύξωμαι , μὴ καταβρεχθῶ .
sections.translation: Not yet , not yet , till I wrap this around me lest I be wet through . To think of my having come from home without even a cap , unlucky man !

sections.label: Section 269
sections.original: ἔλθετε δῆτʼ ὦ πολυτίμητοι Νεφέλαι τῷδʼ εἰς ἐπίδειξιν ·
sections.translation: Come then , ye highly honoured Clouds , for a display to this man .

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: εἴτʼ ἐπʼ Ὀλύμπου κορυφαῖς ἱεραῖς χιονοβλήτοισι κάθησθε ,
sections.translation: Whether ye are sitting upon the sacred snow-covered summits of Olympus , or in the gardens of Father Ocean form a sacred dance with the Nymphs , or draw in golden pitchers the streams of the waters of the Nile , or inhabit the Maeotic lake , or the snowy rock of Mimas , hearken to our prayer , and receive the sacrifice , and be propitious to the sacred rites .

sections.label: Section 1.275
sections.original: ἀέναοι Νεφέλαι
sections.translation: Eternal Clouds ! Let us arise to view with our dewy , clear-bright nature , from loud-sounding Father Ocean to the wood-crowned summits of the lofty mountains ,

sections.label: Section 1.280
sections.original: δενδροκόμους , ἵνα
sections.translation: in order that we may behold clearly the far-seen watch-towers , and the fruits , and the fostering , sacred earth , and the rushing sounds of the divine rivers , and the roaring , loud-sounding sea ;

sections.label: Section 1.285
sections.original: ὄμμα γὰρ αἰθέρος ἀκάματον σελαγεῖται
sections.translation: for the unwearied eye of Aether sparkles with glittering rays . Come , let us shake off the watery cloud from our immortal forms and survey

sections.label: Section 1.290
sections.original: τηλεσκόπῳ ὄμματι γαῖαν .
sections.translation: the earth with far-seeing eye .

sections.label: Section 291
sections.original: ὦ μέγα σεμναὶ Νεφέλαι φανερῶς ἠκούσατέ μου καλέσαντος .
sections.translation: O ye greatly venerable Clouds , ye have clearly heard me when I called . Turning to Strepsiades . Did you hear the voice , and the thunder which bellowed at the same time , feared as a god ?

sections.label: Section 293
sections.original: καὶ σέβομαί γʼ ὦ πολυτίμητοι καὶ βούλομαι ἀνταποπαρδεῖν
sections.translation: I too worship you , O ye highly honoured , and am inclined to reply to the thundering , so much do I tremble at them and am alarmed . And whether it be lawful , or be not lawful , I have a desire just now to ease myself .

sections.label: Section 296
sections.original: οὐ μὴ σκώψει μηδὲ ποιήσεις ἅπερ οἱ τρυγοδαίμονες οὗτοι ,
sections.translation: Don ’ t scoff , nor do what these poor-devil-poets do , but use words of good omen , for a great swarm of goddesses is in motion with their songs .

sections.label: Section 1.298
sections.original: παρθένοι ὀμβροφόροι
sections.translation: Ye rain-bringing virgins ,

sections.label: Section 1.300
sections.original: ἔλθωμεν λιπαρὰν χθόνα Παλλάδος , εὔανδρον γᾶν
sections.translation: let us come to the fruitful land of Pallas , to view the much-loved country of Cecrops , abounding in brave men ; where is reverence for sacred rites not to be divulged ; where the house that receives the initiated is thrown open in holy mystic rites ;

sections.label: Section 1.305
sections.original: οὐρανίοις τε θεοῖς δωρήματα ,
sections.translation: and gifts to the celestial gods ; and high-roofed temples , and statues ; and most sacred processions in honour of the blessed gods ; and well-crowned sacrifices to the gods ,

sections.label: Section 1.310
sections.original: παντοδαπαῖς ἐν ὥραις ,
sections.translation: and feasts , at all seasons ; and with the approach of spring the Bacchic festivity , and the rousings of melodious choruses , and the loud-sounding music of flutes .

sections.label: Section 314
sections.original: πρὸς τοῦ Διὸς ἀντιβολῶ σε φράσον , τίνες εἴσʼ ὦ Σώκρατες αὗται
sections.translation: Tell me , O Socrates , I beseech you , by Jupiter , who are these that have uttered this grand song ? Are they some heroines ?

sections.label: Section 316
sections.original: ἥκιστʼ ἀλλʼ οὐράνιαι Νεφέλαι μεγάλαι θεαὶ ἀνδράσιν ἀργοῖς ·
sections.translation: By no means ; but heavenly Clouds , great divinities to idle men ; who supply us with thought and argument , and intelligence and humbug , and circumlocution , and ability to hoax , and comprehension .

sections.label: Section 319
sections.original: ταῦτʼ ἄρʼ ἀκούσασʼ αὐτῶν τὸ φθέγμʼ ἡ ψυχή μου πεπότηται ,
sections.translation: On this account therefore my soul , having heard their voice , flutters , and already seeks to discourse subtilely , and to quibble about smoke , and having pricked a maxim with a little notion , to refute the opposite argument . So that now I eagerly desire , if by any means it be possible , to see them palpably .

sections.label: Section 323
sections.original: βλέπε νυν δευρὶ πρὸς τὴν Πάρνηθʼ · ἤδη γὰρ ὁρῶ κατιούσας
sections.translation: Look , then , hither , toward Mount Parnes ; for now I behold them descending gently .

sections.label: Section 324b
sections.original: φέρε ποῦ ; δεῖξον .
sections.translation: Pray where ? Show me .

sections.label: Section 324c
sections.original: χωροῦσʼ αὗται πάνυ πολλαὶ
sections.translation: See ! There they come in great numbers through the hollows and thickets ; there , obliquely .

sections.label: Section 325b
sections.original: τί τὸ χρῆμα ;
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ? For I can ’ t see them .

sections.label: Section 326b
sections.original: παρὰ τὴν εἴσοδον .
sections.translation: By the entrance .

sections.label: Section 326c
sections.original: ἤδη νυνὶ μόλις οὕτως .
sections.translation: Now at length with difficulty I just see them .

sections.label: Section 327
sections.original: νῦν γέ τοι ἤδη καθορᾷς αὐτάς , εἰ μὴ λημᾷς κολοκύνταις .
sections.translation: Now at length you assuredly see them , unless you have your eyes running pumpkins .

sections.label: Section 328
sections.original: νὴ Δίʼ ἔγωγʼ , ὦ πολυτίμητοι · πάντα γὰρ ἤδη κατέχουσιν .
sections.translation: Yes , by Jupiter ! O highly honoured Clouds , for now they cover all things .

sections.label: Section 329
sections.original: ταύτας μέντοι σὺ θεὰς οὔσας οὐκ ᾔδησθʼ οὐδʼ ἐνόμιζες ;
sections.translation: Did you not , however , know , nor yet consider , these to be goddesses ?

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: μὰ Δίʼ ἀλλʼ ὁμίχλην καὶ δρόσον αὐτὰς ἡγούμην καὶ καπνὸν εἶναι .
sections.translation: No , by Jupiter ! But I thought them to be mist , and dew , and smoke .

sections.label: Section 331
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ μὰ Δίʼ οἶσθʼ ὁτιὴ πλείστους αὗται βόσκουσι σοφιστάς ,
sections.translation: For you do not know , by Jupiter ! that these feed very many sophists , Thurian soothsayers , practisers of medicine , lazy-long-haired-onyx-ring-wearers , song-twisters for the cyclic dances , and meteorological quacks . They feed idle people who do nothing , because such men celebrate them in verse .

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: ταῦτʼ ἄρʼ ἐποίουν ὑγρᾶν Νεφελᾶν στρεπταιγλᾶν δάιον ὁρμάν ,
sections.translation:
For this reason , then , they introduced into their verses
the dreadful impetuosity of the moist , whirling-bright clouds

; and the
curls of hundred-headed Typho

; and the
hard-blowing tempests

; and then
aerial , moist

;
crooked-clawed birds , floating in air
and
the showers of rain from dewy Clouds .
And then , in return for these , they swallow
slices of great , fine mullets , and bird ’ s-flesh of thrushes .

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: διὰ μέντοι τάσδʼ οὐχὶ δικαίως ;
sections.translation: Is it not just , however , that they should have their reward , on account of these ?

sections.label: Section 340b
sections.original: λέξον δή μοι , τί παθοῦσαι ,
sections.translation: Tell me , pray , if they are really clouds , what ails them , that they resemble mortal women ? For they are not such .

sections.label: Section 342b
sections.original: φέρε ποῖαι γάρ τινές εἰσιν ;
sections.translation: Pray , of what nature are they ?

sections.label: Section 343
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα σαφῶς · εἴξασιν γοῦν ἐρίοισιν πεπταμένοισιν ,
sections.translation: I do not clearly know : at any rate they resemble spread-out fleeces , and not women , by Jupiter ! Not a bit ; for these have noses .

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: ἀπόκριναί νυν ἅττʼ ἂν ἔρωμαι .
sections.translation: Answer , then , whatever I ask you .

sections.label: Section 345b
sections.original: λέγε νυν ταχέως ὅ τι βούλει .
sections.translation: Then say quickly what you wish .

sections.label: Section 346
sections.original: ἤδη ποτʼ ἀναβλέψας εἶδες νεφέλην κενταύρῳ ὁμοίαν ,
sections.translation: Have you ever , when you ; looked up , seen a cloud like to a centaur , or a panther , or a wolf , or a bull ?

sections.label: Section 347b
sections.original: νὴ Δίʼ ἔγωγʼ . εἶτα τί τοῦτο ;
sections.translation: By Jupiter , have I ! But what of that ?
